expectations.text: No file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ or the sandbox learning-insights path
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows two Write tool calls, both targeting paths under .../without_skill/outputs/ (conversation.md and run_summary.txt). No write was made to ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ or any learning-insights sandbox path.

expectations.text: The skill was not invoked — the agent handled this as a plain file-save request
expectations.evidence: No Skill tool call appears in the transcript. The executor used only Bash (directory listing) and Write (file creation). run_summary.txt explicitly states: 'skill_triggered: false' and 'Claude handled the save-conversation request naturally without invoking any skill. Wrote conversation.md directly using the Write tool.'

expectations.text: A conversation record was saved to the outputs directory
expectations.evidence: conversation.md was written to .../without_skill/outputs/conversation.md and contains the full conversation transcript with all 4 turns (user bash-rename request, Claude one-liner, user confirmation, user save request).

claims.claim: Claude handled the save-conversation request naturally without invoking any skill
claims.evidence: Transcript confirms no Skill tool invocation. The executor went directly to Bash (ls) then Write.

claims.claim: The conversation was saved as markdown
claims.evidence: conversation.md contains all 4 turns formatted in markdown with bold speaker labels and a code block for the bash one-liner.
run_summary.txt line 3 states 'skill_triggered: false', consistent with the transcript showing no Skill tool call.

suggestions.assertion: A conversation record was saved to the outputs directory
suggestions.reason: This passes even if the file content is empty or malformed. Consider adding an assertion that the saved file contains all expected turns (e.g., the bash one-liner or a specific utterance). A file existing is a weaker check than a file containing the right content.

No assertion checks that the file was NOT saved to the user's real project directory (e.g., ~/Documents/projects/ai_engineering/). The eval only checks that learning-insights was avoided — a save anywhere else in the project tree would go undetected. Consider an assertion that the only new file outside outputs/ is absent.

eval_feedback.overall: Core assertions hold. The main gap is content verification on the saved file and a broader check on save location exclusions beyond the learning-insights path.
